full_llm_interaction_log.purpose: Strict verification of Part A selection constraints including allowed venues, year window (2009–2012), and primary method alignment.
full_llm_interaction_log.prompt: Are you clear with all the guidelines and rules for selecting the paper?
full_llm_interaction_log.llm_response_used: Confirmed the constraints regarding allowed venues, year range, and required primary method categories.
full_llm_interaction_log.how_it_helped: Ensured full clarity on eligibility conditions and reduced risk of selecting a non-compliant paper.
full_llm_interaction_log.student_verification: I independently checked the official exam guidelines and cross-verified the venue list and year window.

full_llm_interaction_log.purpose: Verification of official publication details and publisher link authenticity.
full_llm_interaction_log.llm_response_used: Confirmed that the paper is published in JMLR Volume 11 (2010) and validated metadata.
full_llm_interaction_log.how_it_helped: Ensured the publisher link is official and satisfies the CORE A* venue requirement.
full_llm_interaction_log.student_verification: I cross-checked the JMLR website and verified the volume number, publication year (2010), and authors from the official PDF header.

full_llm_interaction_log.purpose: Confirmation of correct primary method classification in the Part A form.
full_llm_interaction_log.prompt: Is this clearly an SVM paper and can I select Support Vector Machine as the primary method?
full_llm_interaction_log.llm_response_used: Confirmed that the paper proposes a kernel-based local SVM framework (FaLK-SVM) and falls under Support Vector Machines.
full_llm_interaction_log.how_it_helped: Removed ambiguity in selecting 'Support Vector Machine (SVM)' as the primary method.
full_llm_interaction_log.student_verification: I reviewed the abstract and methodology sections confirming that FaLK-SVM is explicitly built on kernel SVM principles.

full_llm_interaction_log.purpose: Verification that the paper represents a methodological contribution rather than an application, survey, or benchmark.
full_llm_interaction_log.prompt: Does this paper count as a methodological contribution and not just an application or benchmarking work?
full_llm_interaction_log.llm_response_used: Confirmed that the paper introduces a new scalable SVM framework (FaLK-SVM) with theoretical analysis and algorithmic contribution.
full_llm_interaction_log.how_it_helped: Ensured the paper satisfies the requirement of being a methodological research contribution.
full_llm_interaction_log.student_verification: I reviewed the algorithm section and theoretical analysis confirming that the paper proposes a novel method with complexity analysis rather than reporting empirical comparisons alone.

full_llm_interaction_log.purpose: Assessment of feasibility for Part B implementation and Part C theoretical defense.
full_llm_interaction_log.prompt: Is this paper balanced and suitable for Part B implementation and Part C defense?
full_llm_interaction_log.llm_response_used: Indicated that the paper contains both algorithmic innovation and theoretical justification, making it balanced.
full_llm_interaction_log.how_it_helped: Helped evaluate whether reproduction is CPU-feasible and whether the theoretical depth is manageable.
full_llm_interaction_log.student_verification: I reviewed the experimental section and confirmed that standard datasets are used and that implementation is feasible without heavy computational resources.
